name: Palestinian Sumac Eggs
steps: Saute the onion in the oil until deeply caramelized, about 12-15 minutes.
Toss with the sumac.
Fry the eggs separately and serve together with warm bread.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion in the oil over medium-low heat, 15-18 minutes, until deeply caramelized and jammy.
Toss with the sumac off the heat, for its bright, tangy flavor.
Fry the eggs in the same pan to your liking, and serve together with warm bread - sumac and caramelized onion is a foundational Palestinian flavor pairing.

name: Palestinian Labneh with Za'atar

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain
ingredients.note: strained overnight, as a labneh stand-in

steps: Strain the yogurt overnight to thicken it into labneh.
Spread onto a plate, drizzle with the oil, and sprinkle generously with za'atar.
Serve with warm bread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain
ingredients_full.note: strained overnight, as a labneh stand-in

steps_detailed: Strain the yogurt in a cheesecloth-lined sieve overnight in the fridge until thick and spreadable.
Spread onto a plate, drizzle generously with the oil, and sprinkle heavily with za'atar.
Serve with olives and warm bread - labneh and za'atar together are a Palestinian breakfast staple.

name: Palestinian Ful with Olive Oil
steps: Warm the beans with a splash of their liquid.
Mash lightly and stir in the lime juice and garlic.
Drizzle generously with the oil and serve with warm bread.
steps_detailed: Warm the beans with a splash of their liquid over medium-low heat, 5-6 minutes.
Mash lightly, leaving some texture, and stir in the lime juice and garlic.
Top with the diced tomatoes and a dusting of sumac, and drizzle generously with olive oil.
Ful is a breakfast fixture across Palestine, eaten with warm khubz bread.

name: Palestinian Msakhan Rolls
steps: Saute the onion until deeply caramelized, about 15 minutes, and toss with the sumac.
Mix with the shredded chicken.
Roll into the flatbread and serve warm.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion in the oil over medium-low heat, 15-20 minutes, until deeply caramelized and jammy, then toss with the sumac.
Mix the caramelized onion with the shredded chicken.
Roll into warm flatbread, top with the pistachios, and serve - a bite-sized take on musakhan, Palestine's most iconic dish.

name: Palestinian Maftoul
steps: Simmer the chicken with the onion in the broth until tender, about 20-25 minutes.
Serve the shredded chicken and broth over the cooked noodles.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the onion, garlic, and turmeric in the broth for 22-28 minutes until tender, then shred.

Serve the shredded chicken and fragrant broth spooned generously over the noodles (standing in for maftoul, hand-rolled Palestinian couscous pearls) - maftoul is one of Palestine's most distinctive staple grains.

name: Palestinian Fattoush
steps: Toss the cabbage, cucumber, and tomatoes with the sumac and a squeeze of lime.
Top with crumbled toasted bread.
Serve chilled.
steps_detailed: Toss the shredded cabbage, cucumber, and tomatoes with the sumac, lime juice, and oil.
Top with crumbled toasted flatbread for crunch.
Chill briefly and serve - fattoush's generous sumac is a defining Palestinian touch.

name: Palestinian Mjadara
steps: Simmer the split peas in water until nearly tender, about 20 minutes, then add the rice and simmer 15-18 more minutes.
Meanwhile, saute the onion in the oil until deeply caramelized, 15-18 minutes.
Top the lentil-rice mixture with the caramelized onion and serve.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Simmer the split peas (traditionally brown or green lentils) in 2.5 cups water with the cumin for 20-25 minutes until nearly tender.
Add the rice and simmer 18-22 more minutes until both are tender and most of the liquid is absorbed.
Meanwhile, saute the onion in the oil over medium-low heat, 18-22 minutes, until deeply caramelized and almost jammy.

Top the lentil-rice mixture generously with the caramelized onion, and serve with a spoonful of yogurt if you'd like - mjadara, humble and deeply comforting, is a beloved staple across Palestinian households.

name: Palestinian Musakhan
steps: Roast the chicken at 400F for 30-35 minutes until cooked through.
Saute the onion until deeply caramelized, about 15-18 minutes, and toss with the sumac.
Serve the chicken over the flatbread, topped with the sumac onions.
steps_detailed: Roast the chicken at 400F (200C) for 32-38 minutes until deeply browned and cooked through.
Saute the onion in the oil over medium-low heat, 18-22 minutes, until very deeply caramelized and jammy, then toss with the sumac and lime juice.
Lay the flatbread on a platter, top with the sumac onions, then the roasted chicken, and scatter with pistachios.
Musakhan is widely considered Palestine's national dish - the deep, tangy sumac and slow-caramelized onion define it completely.

name: Palestinian Rummaniyeh
steps: Simmer the split peas in water until nearly tender, about 25 minutes.
Add the eggplant, pomegranate juice, and garlic, and simmer 15-18 more minutes.
Serve with rice.
steps_detailed: Simmer the split peas (traditionally lentils) in 2.5 cups water for 25-30 minutes until nearly tender.
Add the eggplant, pomegranate juice, garlic, and oil, and simmer 15-20 more minutes until the eggplant is tender and the flavors have melded.
Finish with the lime juice and serve with rice - rummaniyeh, a tangy lentil and eggplant stew flavored with pomegranate, is a specialty of Gaza.

name: Palestinian Freekeh with Chicken
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Add the bulgur and broth, and simmer, covered, 18-20 minutes until the grain is tender and the chicken is cooked through.
Serve hot.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the onion and garlic over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the bulgur (standing in for freekeh, smoky roasted green wheat) and chicken broth.
Cover and simmer 18-22 minutes until the grain is tender and the chicken is cooked through.
Top with the pistachios and serve - freekeh is a treasured, distinctly smoky Palestinian staple grain.

name: Palestinian Grilled Lamb with Sumac
steps: Mix the lamb with the sumac and garlic, and shape into small patties.
Grill or pan-fry, 4-5 minutes per side, until cooked through.
Finish with the lime juice and serve with rice.
steps_detailed: Mix the lamb thoroughly with the sumac, garlic, and grated onion, and shape into small patties.
Grill or pan-fry in the oil, 4-6 minutes per side, until well charred and cooked through.
Finish with a generous squeeze of lime juice and serve with rice or flatbread - sumac's tang defines much of Palestinian grilled meat cooking.

name: Palestinian Hummus

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, and garlic with a splash of water until smooth.
Drizzle with olive oil and serve with warm bread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, garlic, and oil with a splash of water until very smooth.
Spread onto a plate, drizzle with more oil, and serve with warm bread - hummus is a Palestinian mezze fixture.

name: Palestinian Falafel

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

ingredients.note: cup, chopped, as a parsley stand-in

steps: Blend the chickpeas, garlic, and herbs into a coarse, moldable paste.
Shape into small balls and fry until deeply golden, about 3-4 minutes per side.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

ingredients_full.note: cup, chopped, as a parsley stand-in

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, garlic, herbs, flour, and turmeric in a food processor into a coarse, moldable paste.
Shape into small balls or patties.
Fry in the oil over medium heat, 3-5 minutes per side, until deeply golden and crisp - falafel is a beloved Palestinian street food staple.

name: Palestinian Roasted Chickpeas with Za'atar

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps: Toss the chickpeas with the oil and za'atar.
Roast at 425F for 20-25 minutes, shaking occasionally, until crisp.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps_detailed: Pat the chickpeas very dry with a towel.
Toss with the oil and za'atar.
Roast at 425F (220C) for 22-28 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally, until deeply golden and crisp.

name: Palestinian Msabbaha

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Warm the chickpeas gently with a splash of their liquid, keeping them whole rather than blending.
Whisk the tahini, lime juice, and garlic with a splash of water into a smooth sauce.
Spoon the sauce over the warm whole chickpeas and serve with bread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Warm the chickpeas gently in a splash of their liquid over low heat, 4-5 minutes, keeping them whole.
Whisk the tahini, lime juice, and garlic with a splash of water into a smooth, pourable sauce.

Spoon the tahini sauce generously over the warm whole chickpeas, dust with paprika, and serve with warm bread - msabbaha, warm and chunky rather than smooth, is a distinct cousin of hummus popular for breakfast or a light meal.

name: Palestinian Knafeh Nabulsieh

ingredients.note: crumbled, as a Nabulsi cheese stand-in

steps: Toss the semolina with the melted butter and press half into a small pan.
Top with the feta, then the remaining semolina.
Bake at 375F for 25-30 minutes until golden, then drizzle with the honey.

ingredients_full.note: crumbled, as a Nabulsi cheese stand-in

steps_detailed: Toss the semolina thoroughly with the melted butter until evenly coated.

Press half into a small buttered pan, top with the crumbled feta (standing in for Nabulsi cheese, the mild, semi-firm cheese traditionally used), then cover with the remaining semolina, pressing down firmly.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 26-32 minutes until deeply golden.
Drizzle generously with the honey and scatter with pistachios while still warm - knafeh Nabulsieh, from the city of Nablus, is considered the finest version of this beloved Levantine dessert.

name: Palestinian Date Ma'amoul
steps: Mix the flour, butter, and sugar into a soft, crumbly dough.
Flatten portions, fill with the mashed dates, and reshape into small rounds.
Bake at 350F for 18-22 minutes until just set.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, butter, and sugar into a soft, slightly crumbly dough.
Flatten a portion, fill with the mashed dates, and reshape around it, sealing completely.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 18-24 minutes until just set and very pale - a beloved Palestinian holiday cookie.

name: Palestinian Basbousa
steps: Mix the semolina, milk, sugar, and melted butter into a thick batter.
Pour into a small buttered dish and bake at 350F for 20-25 minutes until golden.
Drizzle with honey while still warm.
steps_detailed: Mix the semolina, milk, sugar, and melted butter into a thick, smooth batter.
Pour into a small buttered baking dish and score into diamond shapes before baking.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 22-28 minutes until deeply golden.

Drizzle generously with the honey while still warm, letting it soak into the scored pattern, and scatter with pistachios - basbousa, a simple semolina cake, is beloved across Palestine and the wider region.

name: Palestinian Zeit u Za'atar
steps: Mix the oil and za'atar into a thick, spreadable paste.
Serve with warm bread for dipping.
steps_detailed: Mix the oil and za'atar into a thick, fragrant, spreadable paste.
Serve with warm bread for dipping - zeit u za'atar, oil and za'atar together, might be the single most common Palestinian everyday food pairing.

name: Palestinian Tahini Sauce

ingredients.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Whisk the tahini, lime juice, and garlic with a splash of water until smooth and pourable.
Serve over falafel, roasted vegetables, or grilled meat.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

ingredients_full.note: cup, chopped, as a parsley stand-in

steps_detailed: Whisk the tahini, lime juice, and garlic together, adding water gradually until smooth and pourable.
Stir in the chopped herbs.
This tahini sauce is essential to Palestinian falafel and roasted vegetable dishes alike.
